description: This chart shows the effect of equivalent plastic strain on dislocation density of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel processed by 250℃ warm multiaxial forging. The black square markers represent experimentally measured dislocation density values, while the red dashed line denotes predicted values fitted using the equation ρ = 7.96E14 + k1*(1-exp(-k2*x)). The fitted parameters are k1 = 7.90516E15 ± 5.28171E14 m⁻², k2 = 1.81319 ± 0.42469, with an R-square (COD) value of 0.98554, indicating high goodness of fit. Dislocation density increases rapidly at low equivalent plastic strain, then its growth rate slows down and tends to saturate as strain increases, rising from ~7.96×10¹⁴ m⁻² at 0 strain to ~9×10¹⁵ m⁻² at equivalent strain of 2.3. The obtained fitting parameters are applied to the modified Johnson-Cook constitutive model to predict the plastic flow behavior of the steel during warm multiaxial forging.
